subject: @nicksaraev SHORT-FORM (vertical) edit — companion to saraev-v1 (long-form)

\u26d4 CORPUS DEFECT \u2014 fps is NOT u.finding: ydif_raw.txt is sampled at each video's NATIVE fps, not 30. Three of 19 are not 30fps: MTVpRn8nCP4=24, On_DVAjnkRY=29.97, aIflIQMacV4=60.
\u26d4 CORPUS DEFECT \u2014 fps is NOT u.consequence: a flat t=i/30 corrupts them. aIflIQMacV4 (60fps) read as 26.3s when it is 13.15s — 2x wrong, and every cut timestamp in it was 2x too late.
\u26d4 CORPUS DEFECT \u2014 fps is NOT u.second_order: a fixed 3-FRAME collapse is 100ms at 30fps but only 50ms at 60fps, so the 60fps video's cut count was inflated (10 -> 7 once corrected).
\u26d4 CORPUS DEFECT \u2014 fps is NOT u.fix: read fps per video; t = i/fps; collapse on a fixed TIME window (100ms), not a frame count.
\u26d4 CORPUS DEFECT \u2014 fps is NOT u.caught_by: the Cut Grammar and What-Is-On-Screen analysts, independently. My first pass had the bug.
\u26d4 CORPUS DEFECT \u2014 fps is NOT u.\u2b50 unforced gift: fps partitions the corpus EXACTLY along the date split — all 16 batch videos are exactly 30fps, all 3 stragglers are 24/29.97/60. Independent confirmation the batch shares one delivery pipeline.

sec_per_cut.\u26d4 WARNING: this is a GRAPHICS-LAYER rate, NOT a talking-head cut rate. ~90% of these YDIF events are a graphics/B-roll overlay appearing, not Nick cutting. Comparing it to long-form's 1-per-25.5s (a talking-head rate) compares two different quantities. See GUIDEBOOK Ch.3.

wps.vs_long_form: long-form 3.41-3.94 (median 3.76). 17/19 shorts EXCEED the long-form max -> the wps 'creator constant' in saraev-v1 is FORMAT-BOUND and must be re-scoped.

CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.claim: his shorts mastering is scattered/sloppy (10 LU spread)
CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.reality: THREE tightly-rendered batches at three different gains. Within-batch spread 0.10-0.70 LU — one group is 20x TIGHTER than long-form's celebrated 0.8 LU. The 10 LU lives BETWEEN groups, not within them.

CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.claim: the 16-video batch is one recording session
CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.reality: >=4 distinct set/wardrobe setups inside the batch. It is one DELIVERY pipeline (uniform 30fps), not one session.
compares a graphics-layer rate to a talking-head cut rate — two different quantities. The browser trap, in a new costume.

CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.claim: loudness is a FORMAT DEMAND — the long-form's 0.8 LU 'creator constant' breaks in short-form (10 LU spread)
CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.reality: it does NOT break on format. Era-matched p=0.44, delta 0.067 LU. The 10 LU is an epoch effect confined to the 2024 batch. His current epoch masters to -16 LUFS in BOTH formats — the constant HOLDS.
CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.status: RETRACTED — see GUIDEBOOK Ch.7 (The Transfer Table)
CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.how_caught: the transfer analyst ran an exact permutation test on all C(9,3)=84 era-matched splits rather than comparing block means. The brief (mine) told it loudness breaks; it checked and contradicted the brief.
CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.lesson: I compared a 2024-heavy block against a 2026 block and attributed the difference to FORMAT. Two of the three confounds I had declared up front (era, batch) were the actual cause — I declared them and then reasoned as if they weren't there.

CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.claim: the shorts use word-by-word karaoke captions
CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.reality: NOT karaoke. Phrase cards, median 1.89 words, ONE pre-assigned keyword colour per card, replaced wholesale. The highlight never sweeps within a card (verified at 10fps: 'AGENCY WHERE' holds AGENCY yellow across all 13 frames).

\u26d4\u26d4 LOUDNESS \u2014 DO NOT TARG.the trap: the 19-short median is -19.6 LUFS. Targeting it puts you 3.6 LU BELOW where he actually masters today.
\u26d4\u26d4 LOUDNESS \u2014 DO NOT TARG.why: the 10 LU spread is an EPOCH effect, not a format effect. Era-matched (3 stragglers 2025-04/2025-07/2026-01 vs the 6 long-forms), loudness differs by +0.067 LU, exact permutation p=0.44 — no separation. Straggler mean -15.93 vs long-form -16.00.
\u26d4\u26d4 LOUDNESS \u2014 DO NOT TARG.the spread lives inside the 2024 batch: nmal1JTOrWI -14.4 and tBQRJBKHwKc -14.4 vs sCj0UajQ86M -24.5. Ch.6 found the same from the other end: 5 of 6 cluster-C shorts within 0.10 LU of each other — three tight batches at three gains.
\u26d4\u26d4 LOUDNESS \u2014 DO NOT TARG.\u2705 THE TARGET: -16 LUFS. He is invariant WITHIN an epoch, and his current epoch masters to -16 in BOTH formats.
\u26d4\u26d4 LOUDNESS \u2014 DO NOT TARG.the distinction that matters: speaking rate breaking and loudness 'breaking' look like the same finding and are not. One is the format acting on the man; the other is 2024-Nick and 2026-Nick being different people at the mixing stage.

VERIFIED_CREATOR_CONSTANTS_across_both_f._note: these survived the era-matched control — they are the man, not the format or the epoch

speech_begins_at_0.00s.\u2b50 nuance: true PCM onset 0.03-0.13s; the first 3 windows are -99 dBFS AAC priming, NOT a pause

no_punch_in_zoom_or_scale_ramp.caveat: only 2 verified same-camera cuts exist in the shorts corpus (26 of 30 transitions are B-roll) — max deviation 0.11%

no_music_bed.evidence: three independent kills incl. side-channel collapse of -29.6 dB with speech
no_music_bed.\u26a0\ufe0f one proof struck: Ch6's headline proof (a) — 'two videos reach true digital silence, -inf dBFS, no bed can coexist' — is a FILE-TAIL ARTIFACT. Both windows are the last few hundred ms of the encode (trailing zeros); a music bed would stop there too. The verifier noted Ch6 stated the 'ask what physically produced the samples' rule and then failed to apply it to its own headline. The n=0 verdict SURVIVES on proofs (b) side-channel collapse -29.6 dB with speech and (c) non-tonal static floor spectrum.

no_SFX_on_cuts.evidence: n=30 cuts, cut-minus-control hiss -0.01 dB, 95% CI [-4.5,+4.5]

dead_air_intolerance.value: 1.83s of >=0.15s silence in the ENTIRE 11.6-min shorts corpus; 15/19 have none
dead_air_intolerance.\u26a0\ufe0f UNREPRODUCIBLE: Ch6 claimed 1.83s of >=0.15s silence and 15/19 with none. The verifier re-derived **7.17s and 10/19**. The direction holds (he is silence-intolerant in both formats) but the specific numbers are NOT reproducible — do not quote them.

burned_captions.shorts: 19/19, median 91% of frames, all present by 0.233s

speaking_rate.note: 0.0119 is the CEILING — perfect separation on all 84 permutations. Drop the podcast repost: still p=0.0357
speaking_rate.\u26a0\ufe0f robustness_FAILS: drop the podcast repost (On_DVAjnkRY) and it is p=0.0357 one-tailed but **0.0714 two-tailed — fails 0.05**. The finding is significant on the full 3-straggler sample and FRAGILE to dropping any one of the three.
speaking_rate.disclosure: the chapter reported 0.0119 without stating it was one-tailed. Caught by the adversarial verifier. Two-tailed is the honest number.
speaking_rate.sample: n=3 era-matched shorts vs n=6 long-form. Tiny. The direction is consistent across all 4 sessions, but do not oversell the p.

mic_rig.note: holds era-matched (MTVpRn8nCP4 2026: M-S 21.2, floor -35.6). Zero overlap, 19 vs 6

KNOWN_OPEN_ITEMS: Ch1's framing recipe ('head ~75-80% of frame height, zero headroom, hair clipped') is CONTRADICTED by the frames and by Ch4. Do not build to it.

Ch3 and Ch4 report different transition widths (142/67/12 vs 127/43/51) — both reproduce exactly, but under different methods (run-at-event-start vs event-span). Unreconciled; the book should say which it means.
Ch6's silence census is unreproducible (see dead_air_intolerance).
Face size / eyeline is UNRESOLVED — Ch4's skin estimator failed validation (fired on hands and a stock family photo); no face model on this machine.
